title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
overview: A century before the events of Game of Thrones, two unlikely heroes wandered Westeros: a young, naive but courageous knight, Ser Duncan the Tall, and his diminutive squire, Egg. Set in an age when the Targaryen line still holds the Iron Throne and the last dragon has not yet passed from living memory, great destinies, powerful foes, and dangerous exploits await these improbable and incomparable friends.
reason: Highly anticipated prequel from George R.R. Martin on HBO, leveraging the massive Game of Thrones franchise. It boasts a strong 8.454 TMDB score and 74 Metacritic, indicating high critical expectation.

title: Wonder Man
overview: Simon and Trevor, two actors at opposite ends of their careers, chase life-changing roles.
reason: A new Marvel Studios series on Disney+, featuring a 7.5 IMDb rating from over 27,000 votes, driven by the popular MCU brand and lead actor Yahya Abdul-Mateen II.

title: HIS & HERS
overview: Two estranged spouses — one a detective, the other a news reporter — vie to solve a murder in which each believes the other is a prime suspect.
reason: Generating significant buzz on Netflix with a 7.2 IMDb rating from over 65,000 pre-release votes, bolstered by a popular cast including Tessa Thompson and Jon Bernthal.

title: The Beast in Me
overview: A famous author is pulled into a twisted mind game with her rich, powerful new neighbor — who might be a murderer.
reason: A highly anticipated Netflix drama with acclaimed leads Claire Danes and Matthew Rhys, evidenced by a strong 7.4 IMDb rating from over 61,000 votes.

title: Run Away
overview: A desperate father searching for his runaway daughter gets caught up in a murder case — and stumbles upon secrets which could destroy his family for good.
reason: A new mystery from best-selling author Harlan Coben, consistently popular on Netflix, with a solid 6.9 IMDb rating from over 20,000 votes.
